Throughout the interview, Gelfand emphasizes the importance of mentoring students and fostering intellectual curiosity, describing student development as one of the most rewarding aspects of his career.

did.unittitle: Selected index of segments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 00:15:04–00:16:49 — Academic freedom and curriculum development. Gelfand discusses the autonomy afforded to the founding faculty in developing research priorities, curricula, and the Foundations of Science program.

00:39:09–00:39:36 — Distributed early campus. He describes working between the Downtown Campus, Sama Tower, and the Center for Science and Engineering in Musaffah, illustrating the logistical realities of NYUAD before the move to Saadiyat.
00:57:54–00:58:30 — Commuting between campuses. Gelfand reflects on the daily commute and how the University's dispersed facilities affected faculty and student life.

01:12:23–01:16:30 (approx.) — Center for Astrophysics and Space Science. He discusses the establishment and growth of astrophysics research at NYUAD, major milestones, and the Center's role in advancing scientific research.

did.unittitle: Interview session with Danny Abbas (IT) and Lauren Kata

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Video-recorded session, with Danny Abbas, Director of IT Services and Operation in the IT Department of NYUAD, recorded on September 20, 2024, and facilitated by Lauren Kata, Assistant Librarian for Archives and Special Collections of NYUAD Library. Conducted as part of The Memory Project. The Memory Project is the NYUAD Library's contribution to celebrating the memories, experiences, traditions, stories and community history of NYU Abu Dhabi. Interviews will be preserved as part of the NYUAD University Archives.

In this oral history interview, Danny Abbas reflects on his eleven-year career at New York University Abu Dhabi, tracing his experiences from the University's early years across multiple campus locations to the development of the Saadiyat campus. He discusses his evolving roles in building and expanding information technology services, emphasizing IT's contribution to the University's mission by supporting teaching, research, and campus operations. Abbas describes the collaborative culture that characterized NYUAD's formative years, highlighting the shared sense of purpose among staff, contractors, and vendors as they helped build a new institution. He also reflects on the challenges of rapid campus growth, vendor transitions, and maintaining continuity during the COVID-19 pandemic, emphasizing the importance of adaptability in a dynamic academic environment. Looking back on his time at NYUAD, Abbas characterizes the University as a close-knit community where collaboration, innovation, and service shaped both his professional development and his lasting connection to the institution.

did.unittitle: Selected index segments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 00:04:22–00:07:14 — Early campus culture and institutional mission. Abbas recalls joining NYUAD during its formative years, describing the collaborative atmosphere and the shared sense of building a new global university.

00:08:18–00:09:50 — Building IT infrastructure across multiple campuses. He discusses supporting operations at the Downtown Campus, Sama Tower, and Musaffah while simultaneously preparing the Saadiyat campus.

00:10:42–00:13:12 — Working with contractors, vendors, and New York University. Abbas reflects on integrating external partners into the NYUAD community and balancing local operational needs with University-wide standards.

01:01:36–01:10:00 (approx.) — Evolution of IT services and the COVID-19 response. He discusses how his role evolved over time and reflects on maintaining academic and administrative continuity during the pandemic.

01:29:05–01:33:00 (approx.) — Reflections on leaving NYUAD. Abbas describes what he will miss most about the University, emphasizing the relationships, collaborative culture, and personal meaning of his years at NYUAD.

did.unittitle: Interview session with Nevzat Ertunga "Ertu" Sarisozen (NYUAD Alumni Reunion) and Lauren Kata

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Video-recorded session, with Ertu Sarisozen, NYUAD Class of 2020 Alumnus, and current Global Outreach Specialist at NYUAD, recorded on November 22, 2024, and facilitated by Lauren Kata, Assistant Librarian for Archives and Special Collections of NYUAD Library. Conducted as part of The Memory Project. The Memory Project is the NYUAD Library's contribution to celebrating the memories, experiences, traditions, stories and community history of NYU Abu Dhabi. Interviews will be preserved as part of the NYUAD University Archives.

In this oral history interview, Nevzat Ertunga "Ertu" Sarisozen reflects on his experiences as a student at New York University Abu Dhabi, describing his journey from initially hesitating to attend the University to becoming an engaged member of its academic and campus community. He recounts how Candidate Weekend shaped his decision to enroll, shares his first impressions of Abu Dhabi, and reflects on the independence and experiences that prepared him for studying abroad. Sarisozen discusses his academic interests, memorable courses, and the mentorship he received from faculty, as well as his involvement in student leadership and campus organizations. Throughout the interview, he emphasizes the importance of community, diversity, and lasting friendships in shaping his undergraduate experience. Looking back after graduation, Sarisozen reflects on how his appreciation for NYUAD has deepened over time and expresses gratitude for the personal relationships and formative experiences that continue to influence his life.

did.unittitle: Interview session with Adam Fejes (NYUAD Alumni Reunion) and Lauren Kata

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Video-recorded oral history interview with Adam Fejes, alumnus, New York University Abu Dhabi Class of 2015, recorded on November 22, 2024, facilitated by Lauren Kata, Associate Academic Librarian for Archives and Special Collections, as part of the NYUAD Memory Project. The Memory Project is the NYUAD Library's contribution to documenting the memories, experiences, traditions, stories, and community history of New York University Abu Dhabi. Interviews are preserved as part of the NYUAD University Archives. Brief interruptions to the recording occur at approximately 00:04:20–00:04:35 and 00:09:46–00:09:58 due to interviewer coughing.

In this oral history interview, Adam Fejes reflects on his experiences as a member of New York University Abu Dhabi's Class of 2015, describing his journey from applying to the University and attending Candidate Weekend to becoming an active member of the campus community. He recalls his first impressions of NYUAD, including Marhaba and the University's early years at Sama Tower and the Downtown Campus, before reflecting on the transition to the Saadiyat campus. Fejes discusses his academic experiences as a psychology major, highlighting courses, faculty mentorship, and intellectual experiences that continued to influence his personal and professional life after graduation. He also reflects on his involvement in student organizations and extracurricular activities, emphasizing the importance of community, friendships, and campus life in shaping his undergraduate experience. Throughout the interview, Fejes describes NYUAD as a transformative environment that fostered personal growth, lasting relationships, and a strong sense of belonging.

did.unittitle: Interview session with Otari Kakhidze (NYUAD Alumni Reunion)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Video-recorded oral history interview with Otari Kakhidze, alumnus, New York University Abu Dhabi Class of 2015, recorded on November 23, 2024, facilitated by Lauren Kata, Associate Academic Librarian for Archives and Special Collections, as part of the NYUAD Memory Project. The Memory Project is the NYUAD Library's contribution to documenting the memories, experiences, traditions, stories, and community history of New York University Abu Dhabi. Interviews are preserved as part of the NYUAD University Archives. A brief interruption occurs at approximately 00:03:22–00:03:39 as the interviewee explains that his voice is hoarse after attending the previous day's Defending the Nest basketball event.

In this oral history interview, Otari Kakhidze reflects on his journey from Tbilisi, Georgia, to New York University Abu Dhabi, describing how his early interest in Arabic studies and the opportunity for a globally integrated education influenced his decision to enroll. He recalls Candidate Weekend in 2011 as a defining experience, sharing his first impressions of Abu Dhabi and the strong sense of community that shaped his choice to attend NYUAD. Kakhidze discusses his involvement in the University's inaugural Student Government, reflecting on the collaborative process of establishing student organizations, traditions, and leadership structures during NYUAD's formative years. He also recounts his participation in athletics, including rugby, dragon boating, triathlon, and equestrian sports, and reflects on student life across the University's evolving campuses, from Sama Tower and the Downtown Campus to Saadiyat Island. Throughout the interview, Kakhidze emphasizes the lasting friendships, leadership opportunities, and sense of community that defined his undergraduate experience.

did.unittitle: Interview session with Staff Council AY24-25 and Lauren Kata

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Video-recorded oral history interview with Janice Liu, Karol Tolpa, Anand Das, Farzan Khan, and Brídín Cotton, members of the 2024–2025 New York University Abu Dhabi Staff Council, recorded on April 22, 2025, facilitated by Lauren Kata, Associate Academic Librarian for Archives and Special Collections, as part of the NYUAD Memory Project. The Memory Project is the NYUAD Library's contribution to documenting the memories, experiences, traditions, stories, and community history of New York University Abu Dhabi. Interviews are preserved as part of the NYUAD University Archives.

In this oral history interview, members of the 2024–2025 New York University Abu Dhabi Staff Council reflect on the Council's role in representing staff perspectives and fostering shared governance across the University. The interview explores how Staff Council provides a forum for collaboration among administrative, academic, and research staff while contributing to institutional decision-making through policy discussions, committee work, and regular engagement with University leadership. The participants discuss initiatives that support staff well-being, professional development, and community building, including networking events, educational programs, and opportunities for cross-divisional collaboration. They also reflect on the evolution of Staff Council, describing its role in advocating for staff interests, strengthening communication across the institution, and creating an inclusive environment where diverse perspectives inform University policies and practices. Throughout the interview, they emphasize collaboration, service, and shared responsibility as central to NYUAD's institutional culture.

did.unittitle: Selected index segments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 00:04:20–00:09:50 — Why we joined Staff Council. Each participant reflects on their motivation for serving on Staff Council, discussing professional growth, representing colleagues, and contributing to the University's development.

00:12:47–00:15:45 — Shared governance and institutional decision-making. The participants discuss Staff Council's role in representing staff voices, contributing to policy development, and collaborating with University leadership through NYUAD's shared governance structure.

00:23:00–00:27:30 — Representing a diverse staff community. Council members explain how academic, administrative, and research staff are represented through dedicated committees and discuss the importance of ensuring that all staff constituencies have a voice.

00:25:30–00:28:30 — Improving research support through Staff Council. Anand Das describes efforts to address procurement challenges affecting researchers, illustrating how Staff Council translates staff concerns into institutional improvements.

00:42:00–00:44:30 — Building community through Staff Council events. The participants discuss potlucks, mixers, academic staff gatherings, and Wellbeing Week activities as opportunities to strengthen relationships across divisions and foster a sense of community.

00:45:45–00:47:30 — Professional development and leadership. Council members reflect on leadership training, committee work, and collaborative learning as important aspects of serving on Staff Council.

Citizen Archivist Memory Recordings [IN PROCESS]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Citizen Archivist series of the NYUAD Memory Project includes short, audio-recorded interview sessions with both members of the NYUAD Community and members of the external community who visited campus during one of the University's "Open Campus Day" opportunities. "Become a Citizen Archivist" is an open, walk-in session that has been offered during several iterations of Open Campus Day.
